findings.message: The new test does not assert the file permissions (0o700 on split_dir/items_dir, 0o600 on index.json/item-NNNN.json) that the production code in _emit_audit_prep explicitly sets. The accompanying code path and PR description emphasize these tight perms because per-item files carry the full diff payload (sensitive), so a regression that drops the chmod calls would silently slip past this test. Add asserts on stat().st_mode & 0o777 for split_dir, items_dir, index.json, and the per-item file.
findings.suggestion: After the existing assertions, add e.g.: `assert (split_dir.stat().st_mode & 0o777) == 0o700`, `assert (items_dir.stat().st_mode & 0o777) == 0o700`, `assert (index_path.stat().st_mode & 0o777) == 0o600`, `assert (item_path.stat().st_mode & 0o777) == 0o600`.

findings.message: The --split-to behavior is documented as idempotent ('an existing DIR is cleared first' — implemented via shutil.rmtree before mkdir), but the test only exercises the fresh-dir path. A regression that removed the rmtree call (leaving stale items from a prior run) would not be caught. Add a second invocation against a pre-populated split_dir and assert stale files are gone.
findings.suggestion: Pre-create `split_dir / 'items' / 'item-9999.json'` with sentinel content before invoking audit-prepare, then assert that file no longer exists after the run and only the freshly written items/index remain.

findings.message: The test only exercises a single agent (`--agent security`), so index ordering, the i-indexed item filename padding (`item-NNNN.json` for i>=10), and multi-item dispatch are not covered. A bug in the `enumerate` loop or `f"item-{i:04d}.json"` formatter for multi-digit indices would not be caught.
findings.suggestion: Either parametrize with multiple `--agent` flags (e.g. security + test-quality + complexity) and assert each index entry's `i` matches its filename, or add a second test case that monkeypatches FRAMEWORK_AGENTS to a list of >=2 agents and verifies item-0000.json and item-0001.json both exist with the correct agent names.
